page: Vultr voc-m-2c-16gb-400s-amd is an x86_64 virtual server belonging to the Optimized Cloud Compute family. It features 2 shared vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC-Rome processor running at 2.0 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 16.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Local storage is bundled with a 400 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No GPU accelerators are present on this instance.

Benchmark data reveals a mixed performance profile. The AMD EPYC-Rome processor delivers strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng tests, placing in the top 25%. However, multi-core CPU performance and Passmark CPU scores fall into the bottom 25%, reflecting the shared nature of the vCPUs. Memory bandwidth for large blocks is average at 74.28 GB/sec read and 53.91 GB/sec write, while cached small-block read bandwidth is relatively weak. Database operations, Redis SET performance, and static web serving throughput are also positioned in the lower tiers.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this server offers a high density of memory and NVMe storage relative to its compute resources. The shared CPU allocation represents a clear tradeoff, making it less suitable for sustained, multi-threaded compute-heavy tasks. However, for workloads that require a large memory footprint and fast local storage without demanding continuous, dedicated CPU performance, the resource allocation provides a highly economical profile.

This server is well-suited for memory-intensive but compute-light workloads. Appropriate use cases include caching layers, small to medium database hosting, lightweight web serving, and development environments that benefit from 16 GB of RAM and 400 GB of fast NVMe storage but do not require dedicated CPU cores.
description: The Vultr voc-m-2c-16gb-400s-amd is an x86_64 virtual server from the Optimized Cloud Compute family, featuring 2 shared AMD EPYC-Rome vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of RAM and a bundled 400 GB NVMe SSD, but lacks GPU acceleration. Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU capabilities, though multi-core performance, database operations, and static web serving throughput fall into lower percentiles due to the shared CPU architecture. Memory bandwidth remains average for large block transfers. This configuration offers a cost-effective, memory-dense profile with substantial local storage. It is best suited for memory-optimized, compute-light workloads such as caching, small databases, and development environments.

tagline: A memory-dense virtual server featuring shared AMD EPYC processors and fast NVMe storage for budget-conscious, memory-intensive workloads.

16.0 GB of system memory with 8.0 GB per core
Bundled 400 GB local NVMe SSD storage
Strong single-core CPU performance in benchmarks
Best suited for caching, small databases, and development environments
